Peter.role: Simon Peter — fisherman, disciple, and Pentecost preacher
Peter.summary: One of the Twelve and part of Jesus' inner circle. Impulsive, loyal, and still carrying the wound of having denied Him.
Peter.relation: To Jesus: close disciple, eyewitness to major moments, and often the spokesman among the apostles.
Peter.why: Why he is here: Peter stands near the center of the denial, the empty tomb, the restoration by the sea, the Ascension, and the public witness of Pentecost.

John.role: John son of Zebedee — beloved disciple and eyewitness
John.summary: One of the Twelve and among Jesus' closest companions. He remains near the cross when many others scatter.
John.relation: To Jesus: part of the inner circle; at the cross Jesus entrusts His mother Mary to John's care.
John.why: Why he is here: John is a key eyewitness to the crucifixion, the empty tomb, the upper-room appearances, and the breakfast by the sea.

James.role: James son of Zebedee — disciple and brother of John
James.summary: One of the Twelve and, with Peter and John, part of Jesus' inner circle during key moments of His ministry.
James.relation: To Jesus: close disciple present among the apostles named in the upper room after the Ascension.
James.why: Why he is here: James helps represent the apostolic circle that moves from fear and confusion into obedience, prayer, and witness.

Andrew.role: Andrew — disciple, brother of Peter, early follower
Andrew.summary: One of the Twelve and Simon Peter's brother. The Gospels often show him bringing people toward Jesus.
Andrew.relation: To Jesus: disciple called early in the ministry and present among the apostles in the upper room in Acts 1.
Andrew.why: Why he is here: Andrew gives voice to the apostolic group in the waiting, the Ascension, and the coming mission.

Matthew.summary: One of the Twelve. Formerly a tax collector, he leaves an old life to follow Jesus.
Matthew.relation: To Jesus: apostle named among those who return to the upper room and devote themselves to prayer after the Ascension.
Matthew.why: Why he is here: Matthew helps the wider apostolic chorus in the final quarter, especially the commission, waiting, and Pentecost scenes.

Thomas.role: Thomas — disciple remembered for doubt and confession
Thomas.summary: One of the Twelve. Honest, cautious, and unwilling to pretend certainty before he truly sees.
Thomas.relation: To Jesus: faithful disciple whose skepticism becomes one of the clearest confessions: 'My Lord and my God.'
Thomas.why: Why he is here: Thomas gives voice to fear, disbelief, and the costly move from doubt to worship.

Mary (mother).summary: The woman who bore and raised Jesus. She carries the sorrow Simeon foresaw and remains among the believers in prayer.
Mary (mother).relation: To Jesus: His mother, present at the cross and later named among those gathered in prayer in Acts 1.
Mary (mother).why: Why she is here: this story is not only public history; it is also personal grief, waiting, and faith inside the community.

Mary Magdalene.role: Mary Magdalene — devoted follower and resurrection witness
Mary Magdalene.summary: A faithful disciple who stays close through the darkest hours and goes to the tomb early on the first day of the week.
Mary Magdalene.relation: To Jesus: beloved follower who becomes the first recorded witness of the risen Jesus in John's Gospel.
Mary Magdalene.why: Why she is here: her testimony carries the shock of loss, the empty tomb, and the recognition of the risen Christ.

Joanna.role: Joanna — woman disciple and witness to the empty tomb
Joanna.summary: A follower of Jesus named in Luke among the women who supported Him and later reported the empty tomb to the apostles.
Joanna.relation: To Jesus: disciple from the wider circle of women who traveled with and supported His ministry.
Joanna.why: Why she is here: Joanna helps represent the women whose witness spans burial, resurrection morning, and the prayerful waiting of Acts 1.

Salome.role: Salome — Galilean follower and witness at cross and tomb
Salome.summary: Named in Mark among the women who followed Jesus, watched the crucifixion from a distance, and came to the tomb with spices.
Salome.relation: To Jesus: devoted woman disciple from the wider circle that stayed near through His death and burial.
Salome.why: Why she is here: Salome helps keep the women's witness visible in the crucifixion and resurrection scenes.

Joseph of Arimathea.role: Joseph of Arimathea — respected council member and secret disciple
Joseph of Arimathea.summary: A wealthy and influential man who steps forward publicly after Jesus' death to ask Pilate for the body.
Joseph of Arimathea.relation: To Jesus: disciple from the margins who risks reputation to honor Him when many others are hiding.
Joseph of Arimathea.why: Why he is here: he helps secure Jesus' burial and shows that courage sometimes appears after the crisis breaks.

Nicodemus.role: Nicodemus — Pharisee, seeker, and quiet ally
Nicodemus.summary: A religious leader who once came to Jesus by night and later assists with His burial.
Nicodemus.relation: To Jesus: cautious but real sympathizer whose spiritual curiosity slowly becomes public loyalty.
Nicodemus.why: Why he is here: his presence shows that even within the religious establishment, some were drawn to Jesus and moved by His death.

Cleopas.role: Cleopas — traveler on the road to Emmaus
Cleopas.summary: A follower outside the central apostolic circle, walking home in disappointment until the risen Jesus joins the journey.
Cleopas.relation: To Jesus: disciple and witness whose heart burns as Scripture is opened and Christ is recognized in the breaking of bread.
Cleopas.why: Why he is here: Cleopas brings the Emmaus testimony into the room, widening the sense that resurrection is breaking in everywhere.

Matthias.role: Matthias — chosen to take Judas's place among the Twelve
Matthias.summary: Chosen in Acts 1 from among those who had followed Jesus from the beginning and witnessed the resurrection era.
Matthias.relation: To Jesus: part of the wider company of disciples and selected to be numbered with the apostles.
Matthias.why: Why he is here: Matthias is mentioned during the waiting days because the community is already being ordered for mission before Pentecost.
